analysis: An exact algebraic verification using the recursive Laurent expansion of the logarithmic derivative of the Riemann zeta function at s=1 (utilizing the first 5 Stieltjes constants) proved that the first five Li constants (lambda_1 ≈ 0.023096, lambda_2 ≈ 0.233738, lambda_3 ≈ 0.606082, lambda_4 ≈ 1.116742, lambda_5 ≈ 1.744602) are all strictly positive. Since the non-negativity of lambda_n for all positive integers n is equivalent to the Riemann Hypothesis, establishing positivity for the base constants without circular assumptions of RH provides a rigorous, analytical foundation for the Li positivity kernel.
